Healthcare License
Requirements by State

Board-verified reciprocity guides, compact status, endorsement fees, and processing timelines for every profession and every state.

8 Professions
51 Jurisdictions
400+ State Guides

Select Your Profession

Dietitian & Nutritionist

RDN (Registered Dietitian Nutritionist)

CDR Registration Examination for Dietitians Compact Enacted

Occupational Therapist

OTR (Occupational Therapist Registered)

NBCOT® OTR Exam Compact Enacted

Physical Therapist

PT (Licensed Physical Therapist)

NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) Compact Active

Respiratory Therapist

RRT (Registered Respiratory Therapist)

TMC (Therapist Multiple-Choice) & CSE (Clinical Simulation Exam) No Compact

Speech-Language Pathologist

CCC-SLP (Certificate of Clinical Competence)

Praxis® Examination in Speech-Language Pathology Compact Active

Audiologist

AuD (Doctor of Audiology)

Praxis® Examination in Audiology Compact Active

Pharmacy Technician

CPhT (Certified Pharmacy Technician)

PTCE (Pharmacy Technician Certification Exam) No Compact

Pharmacist

RPh (Registered Pharmacist)

NAPLEX (North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination) & MPJE No Compact

No Matches Found

Try adjusting your search term.

Why Use This Directory

Board-Verified Data

Every fee, timeline, and requirement is sourced directly from official state licensing boards — not scraped from outdated third-party sites.

Real Processing Times

No vague "4–6 weeks" guesses. See actual board turnaround windows so you can plan your start date with confidence.

Compact Status Tracking

Instantly see which states participate in your profession's compact — and which require a separate endorsement application.

About This Directory

State Licensing Reference is an independent resource providing YMYL-compliant licensing guides for healthcare professionals across the United States. We are not affiliated with any licensing board, compact commission, or government agency.

The information presented is for educational purposes only and does not constitute legal or professional advice. Always verify requirements directly with your target state licensing board before making application decisions.
